chapter: "II"

exercise_number: 10
---
**The Grothendieck Group $K(X)$.** Let $X$ be a noetherian scheme. We define $K(X)$ to be the quotient of the free abelian group generated by all the coherent sheaves on $X$, by the subgroup generated by all expressions $\mathcal{F} - \mathcal{F}\prime - \mathcal{F}\prime\prime$, whenever there is an exact sequence $0 \to \mathcal{F}\prime \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{F}\prime\prime \to 0$ of coherent sheaves on $X$.

(a) If $\mathcal{F}$ is a coherent sheaf, we denote by $\gamma(\mathcal{F})$ its image in $K(X)$. Show that $K(X)$ has the following universal property: for any function $\chi$ from the set of coherent sheaves on $X$ to an abelian group $G$, which is additive for short exact sequences, there is a unique homomorphism $\tilde{\chi}: K(X) \to G$ such that $\chi = \tilde{\chi} \circ \gamma$.

(b) If $X$ is integral, the rank of a coherent sheaf gives a function on the set of coherent sheaves. Show that the rank function defines a surjective homomorphism $\text{rank}: K(X) \to \mathbb{Z}$.

(c) If $Y$ is a closed subscheme of $X$, there is an exact sequence
$$K(Y) \to K(X) \to K(X - Y) \to 0,$$
where the first map is extension by zero, and the second map is restriction.
